texto: When she sat down on the tile next to him, unafraid, his kaleidoscope senses drank in the years that had been printed onto her mind before she was old enough to remember, and he told her a story, projecting into her darkness sensations of light and color and shape, butterflies swirling like silk-spun gold out through a window that opened to a big green field in the days before the bomb.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Mel Paisley: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “When she sat down on the tile next to him, unafraid, his kaleidoscope senses drank in the years t…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, alien, love-hurts pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao-noturna e love-hurts.
